title: Luffy Interrupts Laboon’s Grief
description: On pages 15–18, luffy interrupts laboon’s grief as crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain.
scene: Luffy Interrupts Laboon’s Grief. Crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: don't worry about him, he won't die!

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: What is he trying to do...?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Luffy! Just what the hell are you doing!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Our fight has yet to be settled,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: So we must fight each other again!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Your old friends may be dead,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: And once my crew sails around Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We'll be sure to come back here to see you again.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: And then we'll duke it out again!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on luffy interrupts laboon’s grief. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
